chapter_title: Operation of Bicycles & Mopeds, Golf Carts, and Other Low-powered Vehicles

subsections.label: i
subsections.text: at least two wheels in contact with the ground during operation;

subsections.label: ii
subsections.text: a braking system capable of stopping the device under typical operating conditions;

subsections.label: iii
subsections.text: a gas or electric motor not exceeding 40 cubic centimeters;

subsections.label: iv
subsections.text: a deck designed to allow a person to stand or sit while operating the device; and

subsections.label: v
subsections.text: the ability to be propelled by human power alone; and

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: does not include a pocket bike or a minimotorbike.

“Pocket bike or minimotorbike” means a self-propelled vehicle that is equipped with an electric motor or internal combustion engine having a piston displacement of less than 50 cubic centimeters, is designed to propel itself with not more than two wheels in contact with the ground, has a seat or saddle for the use of the operator, is not designed for use on a highway, and is ineligible for a certificate of title under Chapter 501 (Certificate of Title Act) . The term does not include:

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: a motorized mobility device, as defined by Section 552A.0101 (Operators of Certain Mobility Devices) ;

subsections.label: D
subsections.text: an electric personal assistive mobility device, as defined by Section 551.201 (Definition) ; or

subsections.label: E
subsections.text: a neighborhood electric vehicle, as defined by Section 551.301 (Definition) .

history.raw: Acts 2019, 86th Leg., R.S., Ch. 882 (H.B.3171), Sec. 2.18, eff. September 1, 2019.
